messages.content: is there any search engine alternative to google

Yes, there are several search engine alternatives to Google. Some popular ones include:

1. Bing: Microsoft's search engine, which offers similar features to Google and is the default search engine for Microsoft devices.

2. Yahoo: A long-standing search engine that provides search results powered by Bing.

3. DuckDuckGo: A privacy-focused search engine that does not track user data or personalize search results.

4. Ecosia: A search engine that uses its profits to plant trees. Every search contributes to reforestation efforts.

5. Yandex: A Russian search engine that offers services in multiple languages and provides search results for specific regions.

6. Startpage: A search engine that focuses on privacy and does not record any personal information from its users.

These are just a few examples, and there are many other search engines available depending on your specific needs and preferences.
what is the window when i right click on an item in windows 10

When you right-click on an item in Windows 10, a context menu is displayed. The context menu provides a list of options relevant to the selected item, allowing you to perform various actions on it. The options in the context menu may vary depending on the type of item and its location within the operating system or a specific application. Some common options you may find in the context menu include:

1. Open: Allows you to open the selected item.
2. Cut/Copy/Paste: These options are used to move or duplicate files or text.
3. Delete: Deletes the selected item.
4. Rename: Lets you rename the selected item.
5. Properties: Displays the properties of the selected item, providing information about it and allowing you to modify certain settings.
6. Send to: Provides a list of locations or applications where you can send the selected item.
7. Create shortcut: Creates a shortcut to the selected item.
8. Refresh: Updates the content or view of the selected item.
9. New: Provides options to create a new file, folder, or shortcut.
10. Uninstall: Enables you to uninstall applications or programs.

The options within the context menu can vary depending on the specific item and the applications installed on your Windows 10 system.
how to add an item to context menu

To add an item to the context menu in Windows 10, you can modify the Windows Registry. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to do it:

1. Open the Windows Registry Editor by pressing Windows Key + R, typing "regedit" (without quotes), and hitting Enter.

2. In the Registry Editor, navigate to the following location:
   HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\*\shell

   Note: If you want to add the item to a specific file type or folder, you can navigate to the corresponding location instead of "HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\*\shell". For example, to add the item to the context menu of folders, the path would be "HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\Directory\shell".

3. Right-click on the "shell" folder and select "New" -> "Key". Name the new key as desired. This name will appear in the context menu.

4. Select the newly created key, and in the right-hand pane, right-click and select "New" -> "String Value". Name the new string value as "command" (without quotes).

5. Double-click on the "command" string value, and in the "Value data" field, enter the command to execute when the item is selected in the context menu. For example, if you want to open a specific program or run a script, you would enter the path to the executable file or script.

6. Click "OK" to save the changes.

7. Close the Registry Editor.

After following these steps, the item you added will appear in the context menu when you right-click on files, folders, or the specified item type.

Note: Modifying the Windows Registry can have unintended consequences if not done correctly. It's always a good idea to create a backup of the registry or create a system restore point before making any changes.
can you give me full step to add an item to context menu to open command prompt at the folder location

Certainly! Here is a detailed step-by-step guide to adding an item to the context menu that opens Command Prompt at the folder location:

1. Open the Windows Registry Editor by pressing Windows Key + R, typing "regedit" (without quotes), and hitting Enter.

2. In the Registry Editor, navigate to the following location:
   HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\Directory\Background\shell

3. Right-click on the "shell" folder and select "New" -> "Key". Name the new key as "CommandPrompt" (or any desired name).

4. Select the newly created "CommandPrompt" key, and in the right-hand pane, right-click and select "New" -> "String Value". Name the new string value as "Icon" (without quotes).

5. Double-click on the "Icon" string value, and in the "Value data" field, enter the path to the Command Prompt icon. You can use the default Command Prompt icon located at:
   %SystemRoot%\System32\cmd.exe

6. Right-click on the "CommandPrompt" key, select "New" -> "Key", and name it as "command".

7. Select the newly created "command" key, and in the right-hand pane, double-click on the "(Default)" string value.

8. In the "Value data" field, enter the following command to open Command Prompt at the folder location:
   cmd.exe /s /k pushd "%V"

9. Click "OK" to save the changes.

10. Close the Registry Editor.

After following these steps, you should see the "CommandPrompt" option in the context menu when you right-click on a folder location. Selecting this option will open Command Prompt directly at the folder location you right-clicked on.

Again, remember to exercise caution when modifying the Windows Registry, and consider creating a backup or restore point before making any changes.
i want to know the singer name of a song name "the scientist", this song has phase "i'm not crazy"

"The Scientist" is a song by the British rock band Coldplay. The song's lyrics include the line "I'm not crazy." It was released as the second single from their second studio album, "A Rush of Blood to the Head," in 2002. The lead vocalist and singer for Coldplay is Chris Martin.
